tossups.question: This author wrote a novel in which the title character makes the farmer boy Landry dance with her in exchange for helping him find his sickly twin brother, Sylvinet, whom she cures at the end of the novel. In another novel, this author created a character who happens to meet Haydn while on the run to Vienna. That title protagonist teaches music while living in the “Castle of the Giants” owned by the Rudolstadt family. This author wrote of the creole maid (*) Noun who drowns herself after finding out she’s pregnant with Raymon’s child in the novel Indiana, as well as a work that describes the “incredible slowness” of the agriculture conducted in the title location that the author visits with her tubercular partner. Little Fadette and Consuelo were written by, for 10 points, what one-time partner of Frederic Chopin and author of the travel novel A Winter in Majorca?
tossups.question_sanitized: This author wrote a novel in which the title character makes the farmer boy Landry dance with her in exchange for helping him find his sickly twin brother, Sylvinet, whom she cures at the end of the novel. In another novel, this author created a character who happens to meet Haydn while on the run to Vienna. That title protagonist teaches music while living in the "Castle of the Giants" owned by the Rudolstadt family. This author wrote of the creole maid (*) Noun who drowns herself after finding out she's pregnant with Raymon's child in the novel Indiana, as well as a work that describes the "incredible slowness" of the agriculture conducted in the title location that the author visits with her tubercular partner. Little Fadette and Consuelo were written by, for 10 points, what one-time partner of Frederic Chopin and author of the travel novel A Winter in Majorca?
tossups.answer: George Sand [accept Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand [accept Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]

tossups.question: This author’s unflattering depiction of an ex-lover in She and He prompted that lover’s brother to write an angry response novel called He and She. A character created by this author claims to have tried to steal business secrets after falling from a wall while attempting to meet a maid. Sylvinet joins the army after falling in love with his twin brother’s wife in a novel by this author who was fictionalised as Brigitte in Confession of a Child of the Century by Alfred (*) Musset (“moo-SAY”). This author of La Petite Fadette (“la puh-TEET fa-DETT”) used Pauline Viardot (“paw-LEEN v’yard-OH”) as inspiration for a Spanish orphan whose talent for singing is discovered by maestro Porpora. Sir Ralph and the title character jump into a waterfall at the end of a novel by this author who novelised her stay at Valldemossa (“vile-duh-MOS-suh”) with Frederic Chopin (“SHO-pan”) in A Winter in Majorca (“my-ORC-uh”). For 10 points, name this author of Consuelo (“con-SWAY-loh”) and Indiana.
tossups.question_sanitized: This author's unflattering depiction of an ex-lover in She and He prompted that lover's brother to write an angry response novel called He and She. A character created by this author claims to have tried to steal business secrets after falling from a wall while attempting to meet a maid. Sylvinet joins the army after falling in love with his twin brother's wife in a novel by this author who was fictionalised as Brigitte in Confession of a Child of the Century by Alfred (*) Musset ("moo-SAY"). This author of La Petite Fadette ("la puh-TEET fa-DETT") used Pauline Viardot ("paw-LEEN v'yard-OH") as inspiration for a Spanish orphan whose talent for singing is discovered by maestro Porpora. Sir Ralph and the title character jump into a waterfall at the end of a novel by this author who novelised her stay at Valldemossa ("vile-duh-MOS-suh") with Frederic Chopin ("SHO-pan") in A Winter in Majorca ("my-ORC-uh"). For 10 points, name this author of Consuelo ("con-SWAY-loh") and Indiana.
tossups.answer: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]

tossups.question: The first appendix to a novel by this author describes the supernatural stories the village hemp-dresser tells on autumn nights. A novel by this author about the title opera-singing zingarella in Venice was first published in a periodical she co-founded, whose name translates to Independent Review. Another of this author's novels implies the blackness of the Creole woman Noun, with whom the title character grew up on Réunion. In an autobiographical novel by this woman, she and her (*) tubercular lover retreat to a Carthusian monastery. This author of Consuelo and The Devil's Pool wrote a travelogue that tells of her lover's composing music in the Balearic Islands. For 10 points, name this pseudonymous French author of Indiana and A Winter in Majorca.
tossups.answer: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]

For 10 points, name this pseudonymous French author of Indiana and A Winter in Majorca.

tossups.question: The English poet Elizabeth Barret Browning wrote two odes to this author, subtitled A Desire and A Recognition. In Jaroslaw Iwaszkiewicz’s (“yaro-swav iva-sh-kevich”) play "A Summer in Nohant”, this author is depicted alongside her kids and her lover. The descriptions of the French colony of (*) Reunion in one of this author’s novels was provided by Jules Neraud as this author had never been to Reunion. That novel follows the titular character Indiana as she falls in love first with Raymon de Ramiere, and eventually her cousin Ralph. This author’s autobiographical travel novel A Winter in Mallorca described her travels with the Polish composer Chopin on the island of Mallorca. For 10 points, name this French author whose works include Consuelo and Mauprat.
tossups.answer: George Sand [accept Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand [accept Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]

For 10 points, name this French author whose works include Consuelo and Mauprat.

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, the protagonist cruelly presents a man with locks of hair that she claims are hers, but which actually belonged to her dead maid. A widower travels to a farm to find a new wife, only to fall for a shepherdess traveling with him, in a novel by this author that inspired the painting Ploughing in the Nivernais (“nee-vair-NAY”). Four pastoral novels by this author are gifted to the narrator by his mother in an early scene from (*) Swann’s Way. A novel by this author of The Devil’s Pool ends with the title woman and her lover nearly killing themselves by jumping into a waterfall after she leaves Colonel Delmare on the island of Réunion. A trip with a tubercular composer inspired her travelogue A Winter in Majorca. The novels Consuelo and Indiana are by, for 10 points, what French author and lover of Frédéric Chopin who wrote under a male pseudonym?
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand ("zhorzh sahnd") [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]

A trip with a tubercular composer inspired her travelogue A Winter in Majorca. The novels Consuelo and Indiana are by, for 10 points, what French author and lover of Frederic Chopin who wrote under a male pseudonym?

tossups.question: This author used the soprano Pauline Viardot as inspiration for one of their characters, an abandoned Spanish girl in Italy who impresses the maestro Porpora. That character also features in this author’s sequel The Countess of Rudolstadt, which directly influenced Walt Whitman. In a novel by this author, the title protagonist leaves Colonel Delmare for her cousin Sir Ralph. This author, whom (*) Gustave Flaubert addressed in letters as ‘Master’, wrote an autobiographical travel novel which describes a stay in Valldemosa Monastery during the rain with their lover. This author of Consuelo also wrote the novel Indiana and the travelogue Winter in Majorca. For 10 points, name this lover of Frédéric Chopin, a nineteenth-century female French novelist.
tossups.answer_sanitized: George Sand ('sond') [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]
tossups.question_sanitized: This author used the soprano Pauline Viardot as inspiration for one of their characters, an abandoned Spanish girl in Italy who impresses the maestro Porpora. This author, whom (*) Gustave Flaubert addressed in letters as 'Master', wrote an autobiographical travel novel which describes a stay in Valldemosa Monastery during the rain with their lover. This author of Consuelo also wrote the novel Indiana and the travelogue Winter in Majorca. For 10 points, name this lover of Frederic Chopin, a nineteenth-century female French novelist.

tossups.question: In a novel titled for this stuff, Carruthers and Davies visit the Frisian Islands and discover a plot in which Germany is about to invade England. In a long poem, an Indian that “turned into [the] invisible atmosphere, [and] kept expanding in the earth” pours his secret sign over this substance. That happens in a part of Canto General about the dictator Jose Francia; whose title refers to the betrayal of this substance. The narrator visits the National Library and places the title object in one of its shelves in a Borges story about a tome with infinite pages that is named for this substance. Niki Jumpei is trapped into living with a woman who digs endlessly through this stuff in a novel by Kobo Abe. Amantine-Lucile-Aurore Dupin wrote the novels Indiana and Valentine while using this word as her pseudonym. For 10 points, give this last name of George, the lover of Frederic Chopin.
tossups.answer: sand [accept The Riddle of the Sands, “The Sand Betrayed” or “The Book of Sand” or “George Sand”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: sand [accept The Riddle of the Sands, "The Sand Betrayed" or "The Book of Sand" or "George Sand"]

bonuses.leadin: This author calls life a “reflex act” and declares “We cannot live, except thus mutually” in a poem simply titled “Love.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This author calls life a "reflex act" and declares "We cannot live, except thus mutually" in a poem simply titled "Love." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author, one of whose poems is addressed from Catarina to a lover who has gone abroad. Another of this author’s poems bids: “Gather the north flowers to complete the south.”
The 43rd poem from Elizabeth Browning’s Sonnets from the Portuguese begins by asking “how do I love thee?” before this five-word statement.

Most of Elizabeth Browning’s love poems are to Robert, but she did write a poem about “desire” for this person, describing her as a “large-brained woman and large-hearted man.” In a poem titled for “recognition” of this person, Browning writes: “Beat purer, heart, and higher / Till God unsex thee on the heavenly shore.”
parts_sanitized: Name this author, one of whose poems is addressed from Catarina to a lover who has gone abroad. Another of this author's poems bids: "Gather the north flowers to complete the south."

In a poem titled for "recognition" of this person, Browning writes: "Beat purer, heart, and higher / Till God unsex thee on the heavenly shore."
answers: Elizabeth Barrett Browning [or Elizabeth Barrett; prompt on Browning] (The poems are “Catarina to Camoens” and Sonnet 33, aka “Yes, call me by my pet-name!”)
George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin de Francueil]

bonuses.leadin: This novel’s author was inspired to write “my Aunt’s story” after hearing her recount a story of a woman who confessed to murdering her child. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This novel's author was inspired to write "my Aunt's story" after hearing her recount a story of a woman who confessed to murdering her child. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the Methodist preacher Dinah Morris was inspired by its author’s aunt. In this novel, Dinah’s cousin Hetty Sorrel mothers a child with Arthur Donnithorne.
Adam Bede is by this author who wrote about Edward Casaubon forbidding Dorothea Brooke from remarrying after his death in Middlemarch.

Eliot defended this French author of A Winter in Majorca in a letter, writing that although “I should never dream of going to [this author’s] writings as a moral code… it is given to [this author] to delineate human passion and its results.”

parts: Name this cross-dressing French author of Indiana, who described her stay with Frédéric Chopin at an abandoned monastery in A Winter in Mallorca.
In this novel by Sand, the title Spanish girl, who was modeled on Pauline Viardot (“vee-ar-DOH”), rejects Count Zustiniani (“zoo-stee-NYAH-nee”) before Porpora sends her to Bohemia.

Porpora helps Consuelo to take up this profession. The “phantom” Erik tutors the Swedish girl Christine Daaé (“dai-ay”), who has this artistic profession, in a novel by Gaston Leroux that inspired a stage musical.
answers: opera singer [or singing; or chanteuse; accept soprano; prompt on musician or performer] (The novel is The Phantom of the Opera.)

bonuses.leadin: Robert Graves's translation of a book by this author appends a "Refutation" by José Quadrado that calls this writer "the most immoral of writers" and "the most obscene of women." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author who recalled a miserable vacation with her consumptive lover in A Winter in Majorca. This author of Consuelo and Indiana corresponded with Gustave Flaubert.
A Winter in Majorca belongs to this nonfiction genre, where the writer chronicles their experiences in unfamiliar places. An example of this genre is Marco Polo's Il Milione.

Anthony Sattin intertwines Flaubert's and Florence Nightingale's travels to this location in a book titled for A Winter On this location. Samara visits the drug addict Anis Zani in a novel titled Adrift On this place.
answers: Nile River [accept A Winter on the Nile or Adrift on the Nile]
answers_sanitized: Nile River [accept A Winter on the Nile or Adrift on the Nile]

bonuses.leadin: Description acceptable. This work complains about the inconvenience of a coup d’état that occurred on the day that the authors have published their first novel. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this gossip-filled work written together by two siblings, until one of them went insane and slowly died from venereal (“vuh-NEER-ee-ul”) disease. It describes zoo animals being served at a restaurant during the Siege of Paris.

The Goncourts describe this author of Consuelo as smoking cigars “like an automaton” and claim that this author only worked from 1 to 4 a.m. The title character moves to a farm in Réunion in this author’s novel Indiana.

The Goncourts initiated a story that this author was actually Flaubert’s son. This author of “Ball of Fat” wrote about Mathilde Loisel (“mah-TEELD lwah-ZELL”) realizing that the title object was fake in “The Necklace.”
answers: journal of the Goncourt brothers [accept diary in place of “journal”; accept diary of Edmund and Jules de Goncourt]

packet.name: C - MIT A, NC State, Waterloo A, Oklahoma A

answers_sanitized: journal of the Goncourt brothers [accept diary in place of "journal"; accept diary of Edmund and Jules de Goncourt]
George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]

parts: Name this largest publishing company in France. Its logo is the letter H.
This author left his job as head of publicity of Hachette after the publication of his novel La Confession de Claude. He is better known for writing the novel Germinal and the open letter J’accuse.
Hachette’s founder, Louis Hachette, sold books written by this author, who adopted a male pseudonym for her novel Indiana. Her novel A Winter of Majorca is about a trip she took with Frederic Chopin.
answers_sanitized: George Sand [or Amandine Aurore Lucille Dupin]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about objects that fascinate the young narrator of Marcel Proust’s Swann’s Way, for 10 points each:
parts: A series of rhapsodic and highly detailed reminiscences of summers at Combray early in the novel are triggered by Marcel’s consumption of tea with one of these cookies.

During one of his reminiscences, Marcel thinks about one of these objects in Combray that he compares to “hands joined in prayer.” While on a carriage ride, a young Marcel jots down a short prose piece reflecting on three of these things he sees in the distance, before resolving never to become a writer.
Towards the beginning of Swann’s Way, Marcel is excited to learn that his mother bought him four pastoral novels by this author for his birthday. This author wrote The Devil’s Pool and Consuelo.
answers_sanitized: church spires [accept church steeples; prompt on churches or cathedrals]
George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]

bonuses.leadin: A Winter in Majorca describes this writer’s vacation with her lover Frédéric Chopin. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French novelist who wrote Indiana and Consuelo.
In this Sand novel, the titular nobleman helps his cousin Edmée escape from his savage relatives, only to extract a promise of marriage from her by threatening rape.

Edmée eventually manages to civilise her cousin Bernard de Mauprat, basing his education on this work by her favourite philosopher. A section of this work titled “Profession of Faith of a Savoyard Vicar” proved extremely controversial upon publication.
answers_sanitized: Emile, or on Education [by Jean-Jacques Rousseau]

bonuses.leadin: This novel contains an avatar of Doug Graebner, in the form of an amateur Assyriologist and retired physician who administers heavy doses of opium to his Chinese servant to test his tolerance. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel whose title character falls in love with the cultured redhead Celia Madden and falls out of love with the Methodist church in upstate New York where he had been appointed pastor.

Theron Ware, from the aforementioned Harold Frederic novel, lusts after Celia even harder when she likens herself to this free-spirited pseudonymous female author, who used Pauline Viardot as the model for her title character Consuelo, and wrote about a noblewoman's affair with her cousin Ralph in the novel Indiana.
This loutish Sinclair Lewis title character, a college football captain who gets ordained as a Baptist minister, and his rival Frank Shallard were both modeled after Theron Ware.
answers_sanitized: The Damnation of Theron Ware [or Illumination]

parts: Name this novel published in 1832, in which the title character is trapped in a loveless marriage with Colonel Delmare and declares her love for Sir Ralph before leaping over the side of a waterfall.
Indiana was written by this French novelist of Mauprat and Consuelo. She had affairs with Jules Sandeau, Prosper Merimee, and Frederic Chopin.
This French author of the plays Lorenzaccio and The Moods of Marianne wrote about his affair with George Sand in his autobiographical novel The Confession of a Child of the Century.
answers_sanitized: George Sand [or Amantine Lucile Aurore Dupin]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The author of this novel used Jules Neraud's travel diary to write its sections about the island of Reunion. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel published in 1832, in which the title character is trapped in a loveless marriage with Colonel Delmare and declares her love for Sir Ralph before leaping over the side of a waterfall.
Indiana was written by this French novelist of Mauprat and Consuelo. She had affairs with Jules Sandeau, Prosper Merimee, and Frederic Chopin.
This French author of the plays Lorenzaccio and The Moods of Marianne wrote about his affair with George Sand in his autobiographical novel The Confession of a Child of the Century.
